mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 9.0%, labor market deterioration signals severe demand contraction and recessionary pressures, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, or the signal loses predictive power after 2010.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in new home sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer confidence and construction sector expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate positive forward returns when RSAFS and HOUST move in opposite directions, or interaction term IC is negative over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during post-recession recovery phases reflects expanding capacity utilization and corporate earnings momentum, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the mechanism reverses direction during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls directly increase aggregate household income and consumer spending capacity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple market cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid acceleration in consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening and margin compression, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Elevated CPIAUCSL growth fails to predict negative 60-day returns, or equity markets price in inflation without drawdowns in more than 60% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with a stable or declining federal funds rate indicates accommodative policy supporting economic expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and FEDFUNDS signal yields negative forward returns when GDP growth exceeds 3% while rates remain unchanged, or interaction IC is consistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in industrial production below the 90.0 index level signals severe manufacturing weakness and supply chain disruptions, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO levels below 90.0 fail to predict negative 5-day returns, or the threshold loses predictive accuracy after 2011.
